title: Rust-facade governed-surface positive-allowlist/parity pin (Q5=BIND-RUST); fill AC-074 Rust measurement
note_on_shas: SHAs below are POST-REBASE — the branch was rebased onto main 3b82260 (which had advanced with the 'Slices 27+33 PROMPTED' commit); rebase was clean/no-conflict (disjoint files: main added prompt files + STATUS, this slice touched none of them).

commits.summary: Slice 27 design memo — governed Rust allowlist (17 app-surface types) vs 20 operator-seam re-exports; P1/P2/P3; AC-074 Rust measurement
RED — governed_surface.rs with a deliberate 'rebuild' verb injected into the allowlist; cargo test -p fathomdb fails P3 + length assertion (catch demonstrated)
GREEN — remove injection (test green); rewrite rust.md governed-surface contract; fill AC-074 :906 Rust measurement (no new AC id)
DOC-INDEX — add interfaces/rust.md + slice-27 design-memo rows; refresh acceptance.md row

findings_addressed: Q5=BIND-RUST: the deferred Rust half of AC-074 governance is now landed (Slice 25 landed Py+TS).
rust.md no longer forward-refs 'pin lands at reserved-gap Slice 27 (not yet executed)'; it states the landed governed-surface contract and remains the OWNER of the Rust facade surface.
AC-074 :906 'Rust-facade measurement is defined at Slice 27' replaced with the actual measurement; Py/TS clauses untouched; no new AC id minted.

tdd_evidence.red_evidence: cargo test -p fathomdb --test governed_surface: t_074_recovery_denylist_absent_from_governed_surface FAILED (found ['rebuild']) + t_074_rust_governed_surface_resolves FAILED (len 18 != 17); t_074_denylist_detector_is_not_vacuous passed.
tdd_evidence.green_evidence: cargo test -p fathomdb: governed_surface 3/3 pass; reexports 1/1; no_recovery_surface 1/1; all green.

rust_allowlist.name: GOVERNED_SURFACE_ALLOWLIST
rust_allowlist.partition_note: 17 governed + 20 operator-seam = 37 = the full lib.rs re-export set; nothing unaccounted for.
rust_allowlist.parity_intent: P2 is parity-IN-INTENT, not membership-identity: Rust is a different consumer contract (type set, verbs are Engine methods) so it is NOT asserted membership-equal to the Py/TS verb allowlist; the shared element is the recovery denylist (single source: src/conformance/governed-surface-allowlist.json).
